title: iit-experiments: validating integrated-information measures against exact IIT-4.0 Φ
description:
A suite of small, reproducible computational experiments in Integrated Information Theory, built on the IIT-4.0 implementation in PyPhi. On systems small enough that exact Φ is computable, the experiments ask, systematically, what tracks integrated information:

  • proxy_audit — empirical proxies (Lempel-Ziv complexity, correlation) do not track exact Φ;
  • candidate_audit — theoretically-motivated candidate measures (whole-minus-sum Φ, stochastic interaction, causal density, …) track Φ only moderately;
  • structure_suite — scalar Φ is nearly orthogonal to the cause-effect structure it summarizes;
  • learned_surrogate — a model combining cheap features recovers Φ (detection AUC 0.90) and extrapolates to larger unseen systems for detection but not magnitude; includes a reusable exact-IIT-4.0 feature dataset;
  • emergence_vs_phi — Hoel's causal emergence is nearly orthogonal to Φ;
  • phiid_vs_phi — estimating Φ the data-style way (Integrated Information Decomposition via phyid) roughly halves how well it tracks exact Φ;
  • consciousness_range — a philosophical companion: assuming IIT is true, systems with identical Φ can have cause-effect structures differing 500-fold, so a one-number 'level of consciousness' radically underdetermines the mind.

Includes a synthesis, a preprint-style manuscript, and reusable exact-Φ datasets. See the repository README and SYNTHESIS.md.
